metadata.title: Build a Context Cost Type Breakdown Tool with Claude

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Agentic CLI — build it, measure it, take it apart.

My session is getting slow is a symptom, not a diagnosis. The five context cost types explain which component is dominating and how to fix it. Fixed: always-loaded system context. Per-task: task description and scaffolding. Accumulated: previous turns and their outputs. Tool-output: MCP function results. Connector-MCP: active tool schemas. Five interventions, one dominant culprit.

shot.notes: five cost type labels stacked as colored segments in a bar: Fixed (dark), Per-task (medium), Accumulated (red-tinted), Tool-output (orange), Connector-MCP (yellow); caption: 'slow session = one of five culprits'

beats.narration_text: Ask for the breakdown tool. In the terminal: claude — analyze this session log, classify each token cost into one of the five types, estimate token counts for each, identify the highest reduction potential, and recommend one specific action.

props.command:
claude "Analyze this session log. Classify token cost
  into five types: Fixed / Per-task / Accumulated /
  Tool-output / Connector-MCP.
  Estimate counts. Identify highest reduction
  potential. Recommend one specific action." < session_log.md

beats.narration_text: Read the breakdown script. It parses a session log looking for five markers: system prompt blocks for Fixed, task description sections for Per-task, conversation turn separators for Accumulated, tool call results for Tool-output, and the MCP schema loading section for Connector-MCP. Then it estimates token counts and flags the dominant type.

props.code:
# context_breakdown.py — Claude Code output
import re, json, pathlib

TYPES = ['Fixed','Per-task','Accumulated','Tool-output','Connector-MCP']
PATTERNS = {
    'Fixed':         r'<system>.*?</system>',
    'Per-task':      r'<task>.*?</task>',
    'Accumulated':   r'<turn id=',
    'Tool-output':   r'<tool_result>',
    'Connector-MCP': r'<mcp_schema>',
}

def classify(log_text):
    costs = {}
    for t, pat in PATTERNS.items():
        matches = re.findall(pat, log_text, re.DOTALL)
        costs[t] = sum(len(m.split()) * 1.33 for m in matches)
    dominant = max(costs, key=costs.get)
    return costs, dominant

beats.narration_text: The stacked bar chart renders. Accumulated dominates at eighteen hundred tokens — four hours of conversation turns. Fixed is small. Per-task is moderate. Tool-output is medium. Connector-MCP is eight hundred — three active MCP schemas that were never called. The reduction opportunity arrow appears: disable the GitHub MCP server for this task type — its schema is eight hundred tokens and none of its tools were called.

shot.notes: stacked bar chart: five colored segments stack to total; Accumulated segment largest in red; 'reduction opportunity' arrow pointing to Connector-MCP segment; text: 'disable GitHub MCP — 800 tokens, 0 calls this session'

beats.narration_text: Apply the top recommendation. In the terminal: claude — update context_breakdown.py, start a fresh session with only the three state files and no MCP servers connected, and show the new breakdown.

props.command:
claude "update context_breakdown.py:
  -> start fresh session (state files only)
  -> disable GitHub MCP server
  -> show new five-type breakdown"

beats.narration_text: The breakdown after: Accumulated drops from eighteen hundred to zero — fresh session. Connector-MCP drops from eight hundred to zero — no schemas loaded. The remaining cost is Fixed plus Per-task, four hundred tokens. The session starts lean.

shot.notes: before/after stacked bar comparison: BEFORE — large Accumulated + Connector-MCP segments, total 3200 tokens; AFTER — only Fixed + Per-task, total 400 tokens; '-87% starting cost'

beats.narration_text: Context cost is not one thing — it is five things that respond to five different interventions. Knowing which type is dominant is the difference between my session is degraded and I can fix this in thirty seconds. Accumulated: start fresh. Connector-MCP: disable idle servers. Tool-output: aggregate before returning. Per-task: tighten the prompt.

shot.notes: teardown card: five cost types in left column, intervention in right column — Accumulated: start fresh / Connector-MCP: disable idle servers / Tool-output: aggregate / Per-task: tighten prompt / Fixed: architectural decision

beats.narration_text: Your move: export a session log and run this classifier. Find your dominant cost type. Next reel: the agent-friendly CLI design checker — because what the CLI outputs affects every tool-output line in your breakdown.

shot.notes: next steps: '1) export session log  2) run breakdown classifier  3) identify dominant type  4) apply matching intervention'; teaser: 'next: agent-friendly CLI checker'

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Agentic CLI. Brutalist and Educational AI. At nikbearbrown.com.
